command.books.deselectAll.title: Deselect All Books

command.preview.title: Open Preview
command.previewToSide.title: Open Preview to the Side
view.container.title: Japanese Novel
view.books.name: Books
walkthrough.title: Get started with Japanese Novel
walkthrough.description: Write, preview, and build a vertical-writing Japanese novel.
walkthrough.chapter.title: Write your first chapter
walkthrough.chapter.description:
Chapters are plain `.jpnov` files. Create one and start writing — ruby, emphasis dots, and page breaks are Aozora Bunko annotations, highlighted as you type.
[Create a Chapter](command:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile?%7B%22languageId%22%3A%22novel-jp%22%7D)
walkthrough.preview.title: See it laid out vertically
walkthrough.preview.description:
Open the live preview to see your manuscript in vertical, right-to-left genkō-yōshi layout. It updates as you write.
[Open the Preview](command:jpnov.previewToSide)
walkthrough.filelist.title: Collect chapters into a book
walkthrough.filelist.description:
A `.filelist` lists one book's chapters, one file per line, in reading order. Save it anywhere in your workspace folder — entries resolve relative to the `.filelist` itself. One `.filelist` is one book.
[Create a Filelist](command:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile?%7B%22languageId%22%3A%22novel-jp-filelist%22%7D)
walkthrough.build.title: Build your book
walkthrough.build.description:
Open the Japanese Novel panel in the Activity Bar (the book icon), tick the books you want, and build them to paginated HTML or Aozora-format text from the title-bar actions.
[Open the Books Panel](command:workbench.view.extension.jpnov)
walkthrough.highlight.title: Highlight your cast
walkthrough.highlight.description:
Add your cast and coined words to settings — they light up in the narration as you write. A name is coloured where it reads as the subject (`巳一は`); keywords are bolded everywhere.
[Open Highlight Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22jpnov.highlight%22)
jpnov.lint.title: Japanese Novel — Lint
jpnov.lint.common.sentenceLength.descrip: Warn when a sentence is longer than this many characters. Recommended: 100. Leave empty to disable.
jpnov.lint.common.maxTen.description: Warn when a sentence has more than this many commas (、). Recommended: 3. Leave empty to disable.
jpnov.lint.common.maxKanjiRun.descriptio: Warn when this many kanji appear in a row. Recommended: 6. Leave empty to disable.
jpnov.lint.common.noEmDash.description: Flag a single em dash (—); novels use a double dash (――).
jpnov.lint.common.noNfd.description: Flag decomposed (NFD) characters; prefer the composed (NFC) form.
jpnov.lint.common.noZeroWidth.descriptio: Flag zero-width spaces and similar invisible characters.
jpnov.lint.common.jaNoSpaceBetweenFullWi: Flag a half-width space between two full-width characters. Auto-fixable.
jpnov.lint.common.jaUnnaturalAlphabet.de: Flag unnatural runs of half-width letters (likely typos).
jpnov.lint.common.minusPosition.descript: Flag a minus sign that is not directly before a number.
jpnov.lint.narration.generalNovelStyle.d: Check Japanese novel paragraph style — a paragraph should start with a full-width indent (　) or an opening bracket. Auto-fixable.
jpnov.lint.narration.jaNoMixedPeriod.des: Flag a narration sentence that does not end with 。 (― and … are allowed). Auto-fixable.
jpnov.lint.ruby.kana.description: Require every ruby reading 《…》 to be all one kana type (this also rejects half-width kana and NFD).
jpnov.layout.title: Japanese Novel — Layout
jpnov.preview.title: Japanese Novel — Preview
jpnov.html.title: Japanese Novel — HTML Output
jpnov.layout.charsPerLine.description: Characters per line for the preview and the HTML build grid. Genkō-yōshi default: 40.
jpnov.layout.linesPerPage.description: Lines per page for the HTML build grid. The preview is a continuous flow and ignores this. Genkō-yōshi default: 34.
jpnov.layout.avoidLineBreaks.description: Kinsoku shori: keep closing brackets/punctuation off a line start and opening brackets off a line end. Applies to both the preview and the built book.
jpnov.preview.lineNumbers.description: Show a small line number in the head margin of each line in the preview.
jpnov.preview.edgeLine.description: Draw a ruled line between lines (like manuscript paper) in the preview.
jpnov.html.lineNumbers.description: Show a small line number in the head margin of each line in the built HTML.
jpnov.html.edgeLine.description: Draw a ruled line between lines and a matching border around each page in the built HTML.
jpnov.html.pageNumber.position.descripti: Where to place the page number in the built HTML (vertical, right-bound book).
jpnov.html.header.description: Header text centered at the top of every page in the built HTML. Leave empty for none.
jpnov.project.title: Japanese Novel — Project
jpnov.project.outDir.description: Folder (relative to the workspace folder) where built `.html` / `.txt` books are written. This folder is never scanned for `.filelist` books. Default: `dist`.
jpnov.highlight.title: Japanese Novel — Highlighting
jpnov.highlight.characters.description: Your cast, one name per item (e.g. `朝霧　巳一`). A name is split on the half-/full-width space into surname + given, and the full name, the surname alone, and the given name alone are each recognised. Highlighted only in narration where it reads as a subject — the name (one honorific such as `さん` / `先生` allowed) directly followed by `は` or `が`. Dialogue is never coloured. Empty and duplicate items are ignored.
jpnov.highlight.keywords.description: Coined terms — a fantasy noun, a place name, … — one per item. Bolded wherever they appear in narration, without changing colour. Where a term is also a character, the character subject wins (`境無は` is the character; a bare `境無` is a keyword). Empty and duplicate items are ignored.
